subtasks.description: Query SAM (Software Asset Management) system for Microsoft licensed users by product, license type, and deployment scope. Extract user counts and license entitlements.
Query consumption facts warehouse for active users by Microsoft product from billing/usage logs. Aggregate active user counts, usage frequency, and deployment patterns.

Reconcile licensed users (from SAM) against active users (from consumption facts). Calculate under-provisioning (active > licensed), over-provisioning (licensed > active with usage threshold), and identify licensing gaps by product and location. Classify each product as: under-provisioned, over-provisioned, or optimally provisioned.

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks required: (1) SAM query (mid tier: multi-table join across product, license, and user entitlement tables), (2) consumption facts query (mid tier: aggregation across billing tables with user deduplication), and (3) cross_recon (frontier tier: complex reconciliation involving two data sources, gap analysis, usage-aware classification logic, and provisioning posture determination). The cross_recon is frontier because it must synthesize licensing rules with consumption patterns, handle edge cases (inactive licensed users, unlicensed active users), and produce a nuanced classification—not a simple comparison.

output.sql: SELECT cf.product, cf.publisher, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS active_users, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS total_active_seat_days, AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_spend_usd, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.license_id) AS license_count, SUM(cf.amount_usd) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id), 0) AS spend_per_active_user, MAX(cf.daily_active_seats) AS peak_active_seats, MIN(cf.daily_active_seats) AS min_active_seats, CASE WHEN AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) > COUNT(DISTINCT cf.license_id) THEN 'UNDER-PROVISIONED' WHEN AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) < COUNT(DISTINCT cf.license_id) * 0.7 THEN 'OVER-PROVISIONED' ELSE 'APPROPRIATELY-PROVISIONED' END AS provisioning_status, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.license_id) - COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS license_user_gap FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher ILIKE '%Microsoft%' AND cf.event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY cf.product, cf.publisher ORDER BY total_spend_usd DESC

SELECT product, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS active_users, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_daily_active_seats, AVG(daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, COUNT(event_id) AS total_events, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_spend_usd, MIN(event_date) AS first_seen, MAX(event_date) AS last_seen, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_type = 'usage' THEN user_id END) AS usage_event_users, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_type = 'billing' THEN user_id END) AS billing_event_users, ROUND(COUNT(event_id)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT user_id), 0), 2) AS events_per_user, MAX(daily_active_seats) AS peak_daily_active_seats, MIN(daily_active_seats) AS min_daily_active_seats, STDDEV(daily_active_seats) AS stddev_daily_active_seats FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher ILIKE '%microsoft%' GROUP BY product ORDER BY active_users DESC, total_spend_usd DESC

output.note: Upstream outputs contain only SQL query strings, not executed results. Cannot reconcile without actual row data from query_sam_licensed and query_consumption_active.
